title: Cooks, Restaurant
description: Prepare, season, and cook dishes such as soups, meats, vegetables, or desserts in restaurants. May order supplies, keep records and accounts, price items on menu, or plan menu.

tasks.text: Ensure food is stored and cooked at correct temperature by regulating temperature of ovens, broilers, grills, and roasters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect and clean food preparation areas, such as equipment, work surfaces, and serving areas, to ensure safe and sanitary food-handling practices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Portion, arrange, and garnish food, and serve food to waiters or patrons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Ensure freshness of food and ingredients by checking for quality, keeping track of old and new items, and rotating stock.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Season and cook food according to recipes or personal judgment and experience.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Coordinate and supervise work of kitchen staff.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Bake, roast, broil, and steam meats, fish, vegetables, and other foods.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Weigh, measure, and mix ingredients according to recipes or personal judgment, using various kitchen utensils and equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn or stir foods to ensure even cooking.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe and test foods to determine if they have been cooked sufficiently, using methods such as tasting, smelling, or piercing them with utensils.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Substitute for or assist other cooks during emergencies or rush periods.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Wash, peel, cut, and seed fruits and vegetables to prepare them for consumption.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Bake breads, rolls, cakes, and pastries.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Carve and trim meats such as beef, veal, ham, pork, and lamb for hot or cold service, or for sandwiches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Keep records and accounts.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Prepare relishes and hors d'oeuvres.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Estimate expected food consumption, requisition or purchase supplies, or procure food from storage.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Consult with supervisory staff to plan menus, taking into consideration factors such as costs and special event needs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Butcher and dress animals, fowl, or shellfish, or cut and bone meat prior to cooking.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Plan and price menu items.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
